message:
feat(middleware): add response interceptors and transformers (fastapi_rust-u5j)

Implements a simplified ResponseInterceptor trait for response-only
post-processing, separate from the full Middleware trait. Includes:

- ResponseInterceptor trait: simplified interface for after-handler processing
- ResponseInterceptorContext: provides request info and timing data
- ResponseInterceptorStack: chains multiple interceptors in order
- TimingInterceptor: adds X-Response-Time and optional Server-Timing headers
- DebugInfoInterceptor: adds debug headers (path, method, timing, request ID)
- ResponseBodyTransform: transforms response bodies with optional content-type filter
- HeaderTransformInterceptor: add/rename response headers
- ConditionalInterceptor: applies interceptor only when condition is met
- ErrorResponseTransformer: hides error details for specific status codes
- ResponseInterceptorMiddleware: adapter to use interceptors as middleware

Includes 14 unit tests covering all functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

All 102 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wmx","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:57:59.677644720Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-1wmx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
Sub-router mounting is essential for organizing large APIs. It allows defining routes in modules and mounting them at prefixes.

## Current Limitation
Router has no mount() method. All routes must be added directly to root router.

## Solution Architecture

### API Design
```rust
let api_v1 = Router::new()
    .route("/users", get_users)
    .route("/items", get_items);

let admin = Router::new()
    .route("/stats", get_stats);

let app = App::new()
    .mount("/api/v1", api_v1)  // All routes under /api/v1/
    .mount("/admin", admin);   // All routes under /admin/
```

### Implementation
Mount merges child routes into parent trie at prefix:
```rust
impl Router {
    pub fn mount(mut self, prefix: &str, child: Router) -> Self {
        for route in child.routes {
            let full_path = format!("{}{}", prefix.trim_end_matches('/'), route.path);
            self.add(route.method, &full_path, route.handler);
        }
        self
    }
}
```

### Considerations
1. Trailing slashes: `/api/v1` + `/users` = `/api/v1/users`
2. Middleware inheritance: Child routes may want parent middleware
3. Path parameter conflicts: Prefixes shouldn't shadow child params
4. Performance: Don't duplicate trie structure, integrate into parent

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Router::mount()
- May need route prefix tracking for middleware

## Dependencies
- None for basic mount
- bd-3s9z (route metadata) for middleware inheritance

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Router.mount(prefix, child) method exists
- [ ] Child routes accessible at prefix + child path
- [ ] Path parameters work in both prefix and child
- [ ] Nested mounts work (mount router that has mounts)
- [ ] Tests verify mounting behavior

 {"id":"bd-20ff","title":"CRITICAL: Enum schema generation in derive macros","description":"The JsonSchema derive macro doesn't properly generate OpenAPI schemas for Rust enums. Enum variants should map to oneOf/anyOf in OpenAPI 3.1. This blocks API documentation for any enum types.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:49:08.904417326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:43:48.584481586Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:43:35.446078674Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20ff","depends_on_id":"bd-12f8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:54:37.159068059Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20ff","depends_on_id":"bd-1t3y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:10.805381065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-20ff","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The JsonSchema derive macro generates OpenAPI 3.1 schemas from Rust types. While it works for structs, enum support is incomplete or missing. Rust enums should map to OpenAPI oneOf/anyOf constructs.

## Current Problem
```rust
#[derive(JsonSchema)]
enum Status {
    Active,
    Inactive,
    Pending { reason: String },
}
```
This should generate:
```json
{
  "oneOf": [
    { "const": "Active" },
    { "const": "Inactive" },
    {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "Pending": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": { "reason": { "type": "string" } }
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

But the macro either:
1. Doesn't handle enums at all
2. Generates incorrect schemas
3. Panics on enum input

## Enum Variants to Support

### Unit variants
```rust
enum Color { Red, Green, Blue }
// -> "enum": ["Red", "Green", "Blue"]
```

### Tuple variants
```rust
enum Result { Ok(T), Err(E) }
// -> oneOf with type discrimination
```

### Struct variants
```rust
enum Message { Text { body: String }, Image { url: String, width: u32 } }
// -> oneOf with object schemas
```

### Mixed variants
Real-world enums often mix variant types

## Solution Architecture

### In fastapi-macros/src/lib.rs
1. Add enum handling to JsonSchema derive
2. Parse enum variants
3. Generate appropriate schema based on variant type
4. Handle #[serde(rename_all)] and other serde attributes
5. Support #[schema()] attributes for customization

### Schema Generation Strategy
```rust
fn generate_enum_schema(enum_data: &DataEnum) -> TokenStream {
    let variants: Vec<_> = enum_data.variants.iter().map(|v| {
        match &v.fields {
            Fields::Unit => generate_const_schema(&v.ident),
            Fields::Unnamed(f) => generate_tuple_schema(&v.ident, f),
            Fields::Named(f) => generate_struct_schema(&v.ident, f),
        }
    }).collect();
    
    quote! {
        schemars::schema::Schema::Object(schemars::schema::SchemaObject {
            subschemas: Some(Box::new(schemars::schema::SubschemaValidation {
                one_of: Some(vec![#(#variants),*]),
                ..Default::default()
            })),
            ..Default::default()
        })
    }
}
```

## Files to Modify
1. crates/fastapi-macros/src/lib.rs - Main macro implementation
2. crates/fastapi-macros/src/schema.rs - Schema generation helpers (may need to create)
3. crates/fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs - Ensure Schema types support oneOf

## Dependencies
- Requires understanding of serde's enum serialization rules
- Should align with schemars crate conventions where possible

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] Tuple variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] Struct variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] Mixed variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] #[serde(tag = "type")] adjacently tagged enums work
- [ ] #[serde(untagged)] untagged enums work
- [ ] Generated schemas validate in OpenAPI validators
- [ ] Tests cover all enum patterns

## Test Plan
1. Unit test: Each variant type in isolation
2. Integration test: Enums with serde attributes
3. Validation test: Schemas pass OpenAPI validator
4. Round-trip test: Schema correctly describes enum serialization

## Considerations
- Serde has multiple tagging strategies (external, internal, adjacent, untagged)
- Each produces different JSON, requiring different schemas
- Should match serde's actual behavior exactly
- Consider generating discriminator hints for code generators

 {"id":"bd-229o","title":"Core: Handler trait definition","description":"Define Handler trait that unifies sync/async handlers. Trait should be the contract between router and handler dispatch. Enables type-erased handler storage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:59:05.519285282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:08:04.578221840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:08:04.578158151Z","close_reason":"Handler trait already exists in middleware.rs (lines 207-237) with blanket impl for async functions. Re-exported from lib.rs. No additional work needed.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-229o","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:59:34.341722524Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-229o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The Handler trait is the core abstraction that allows the framework to store and invoke user-defined route handlers uniformly. It bridges the gap between statically-typed handler functions and the dynamic dispatch needed by the router.

## Why This Is Critical
Without a Handler trait:
- Route struct can't store handlers (bd-3kxd blocked)
- Middleware can't wrap handlers uniformly (bd-3c5c blocked)
- No way to convert user functions to framework-compatible handlers

## Design Requirements
1. Support async handlers (most common case)
2. Support sync handlers for simple operations
3. Work with Cx capability token from asupersync
4. Allow FromRequest extraction in handlers
5. Allow IntoResponse return types
6. Support Clone for handler reuse
7. Be object-safe for dynamic dispatch (dyn Handler)

## Solution Architecture

### Basic Handler Trait
```rust
pub trait Handler: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static {
    type Future: Future<Output = Response> + Send;
    
    fn call(&self, cx: &Cx, req: Request) -> Self::Future;
}
```

### Blanket Implementations
```rust
// For async functions
impl<F, Fut> Handler for F
where
    F: Fn(&Cx, Request) -> Fut + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
    Fut: Future<Output = Response> + Send,
{
    type Future = Fut;
    fn call(&self, cx: &Cx, req: Request) -> Self::Future {
        (self)(cx, req)
    }
}
```

### Type Erasure for Storage
```rust
pub struct BoxedHandler {
    inner: Box<dyn ErasedHandler>,
}

trait ErasedHandler: Send + Sync {
    fn call(&self, cx: &Cx, req: Request) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Response> + Send>>;
}
```

## Integration with Extractors
Handler should work with extracted parameters:
```rust
// User writes:
async fn get_user(Path(id): Path<i64>, Query(q): Query<Q>) -> Json<User>

// Framework sees:
impl Handler for GetUser {
    async fn call(&self, cx: &Cx, req: Request) -> Response {
        let id = Path::<i64>::from_request(cx, &req).await?;
        let q = Query::<Q>::from_request(cx, &req).await?;
        let result = actual_handler(id, q).await;
        result.into_response()
    }
}
```

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-core/src/handler.rs (new file)
- crates/fastapi-core/src/lib.rs (re-export)
- crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs (use Handler)

## Dependencies
- None - this is foundational

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Handler trait defined with call() method
- [ ] Works with async fn
- [ ] Works with closures
- [ ] Type-erasable for dyn dispatch
- [ ] Clone + Send + Sync bounds
- [ ] Integrates with Cx
- [ ] Tests verify handler invocation

 {"id":"bd-323c","title":"Implement validation error display","description":"## Task: Validation Error Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 4: HTTP & Router Integration (bd-1hu2)

### Overview
Create a beautiful validation error display that shows field-level errors, expected types, and nested validation failures in a clear, actionable format.

### Background & Reasoning
Validation errors are one of the most common issues developers face. Clear error messages:
- Pinpoint exactly which field failed
- Show what was expected vs what was received
- Handle nested structures clearly
- Group multiple errors logically

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/validation_error.rs

pub struct ValidationErrorDisplay {
    pub errors: Vec<ValidationError>,
    pub source_type: String,  // e.g., "Json<CreateUser>"
    pub context: Option<String>,
}

pub struct ValidationError {
    pub path: Vec<String>,     // ["user", "email"]
    pub message: String,
    pub code: String,          // e.g., "email", "required", "min_length"
    pub expected: Option<String>,
    pub received: Option<String>,
    pub nested: Vec<ValidationError>,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode (Single Error)**:
```
┌────────────────── Validation Error ──────────────┐
│                                                   │
│  Failed to validate: Json<CreateUser>             │
│                                                   │
│  ✗ user.email                                    │
│    │ Invalid email format                        │
│    │ Expected: valid email address               │
│    │ Received: "not-an-email"                   │
│                                                   │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Rich Mode (Multiple Errors)**:
```
┌────────────────── Validation Error ──────────────┐
│                                                   │
│  Failed to validate: Json<CreateUser>             │
│  3 validation errors                              │
│                                                   │
│  ✗ user.email                                    │
│    └── Invalid email format                      │
│                                                   │
│  ✗ user.age                                      │
│    └── Must be at least 18                       │
│        Expected: >= 18                           │
│        Received: 15                              │
│                                                   │
│  ✗ user.roles[0]                                 │
│    └── Unknown role                              │
│        Expected: one of [admin, user, guest]     │
│        Received: "superuser"                    │
│                                                   │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Rich Mode (Nested Errors)**:
```
┌────────────────── Validation Error ──────────────┐
│                                                   │
│  Failed to validate: Json<Order>                  │
│                                                   │
│  ✗ items[0].product                              │
│    ├── ✗ name                                    │
│    │   └── Required field missing                │
│    └── ✗ price                                   │
│        └── Must be positive                      │
│            Received: -10.00                      │
│                                                   │
│  ✗ items[1].quantity                             │
│    └── Must be at least 1                        │
│        Received: 0                               │
│                                                   │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
Validation Error: Json<CreateUser>
- user.email: Invalid email format (expected: valid email, got: "not-an-email")
- user.age: Must be at least 18 (expected: >= 18, got: 15)
- user.roles[0]: Unknown role (expected: one of [admin, user, guest], got: "superuser")
```

#### Implementation
```rust
impl ValidationErrorDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        // Header
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!(
            "  Failed to validate: {}",
            theme.style_text(&self.source_type, "cyan")
        ));
        
        if self.errors.len() > 1 {
            content.push(format!("  {} validation errors", self.errors.len()));
        }
        
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // Errors
        for error in &self.errors {
            self.render_error(&mut content, error, 0, theme);
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("Validation Error")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
    
    fn render_error(
        &self,
        content: &mut Vec<String>,
        error: &ValidationError,
        depth: usize,
        theme: &FastApiTheme,
    ) {
        let indent = "  ".repeat(depth + 1);
        let path = error.path.join(".");
        
        // Error path
        content.push(format!(
            "{}✗ {}",
            indent,
            theme.style_text(&path, "red bold")
        ));
        
        // Nested or leaf error
        if error.nested.is_empty() {
            // Leaf error
            let connector = if depth > 0 { "└──" } else { "│" };
            content.push(format!(
                "{}  {} {}",
                indent,
                connector,
                error.message
            ));
            
            if let Some(expected) = &error.expected {
                content.push(format!(
                    "{}      Expected: {}",
                    indent,
                    theme.style_text(expected, "green")
                ));
            }
            
            if let Some(received) = &error.received {
                content.push(format!(
                    "{}      Received: {}",
                    indent,
                    theme.style_text(received, "red")
                ));
            }
        } else {
            // Nested errors
            for (i, nested) in error.nested.iter().enumerate() {
                let is_last = i == error.nested.len() - 1;
                let prefix = if is_last { "└──" } else { "├──" };
                
                content.push(format!("{}  {} ", indent, prefix));
                self.render_error(content, nested, depth + 1, theme);
            }
        }
    }
    
    fn display_plain(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        output.plain(&format!("Validation Error: {}", self.source_type));
        
        for error in &self.errors {
            self.render_error_plain(output, error, "");
        }
    }
    
    fn render_error_plain(&self, output: &RichOutput, error: &ValidationError, prefix: &str) {
        let path = error.path.join(".");
        
        if error.nested.is_empty() {
            let mut msg = format!("- {}{}: {}", prefix, path, error.message);
            
            if let (Some(exp), Some(rec)) = (&error.expected, &error.received) {
                msg.push_str(&format!(" (expected: {}, got: {})", exp, rec));
            }
            
            output.plain(&msg);
        } else {
            for nested in &error.nested {
                self.render_error_plain(output, nested, &format!("{}{}", prefix, path));
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Integration with Extractors
```rust
// In fastapi-core/src/extract/json.rs

impl<T: DeserializeOwned + Validate> FromRequest for Json<T> {
    type Error = HttpError;
    
    async fn from_request(req: &Request, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
        let value: T = serde_json::from_slice(req.body()?)
            .map_err(|e| /* parse error */)?;
        
        if let Err(errors) = value.validate() {
            let display = ValidationErrorDisplay {
                errors: errors.into(),
                source_type: format!("Json<{}>", std::any::type_name::<T>()),
                context: None,
            };
            display.display();  // Only displays if debug enabled
            
            return Err(HttpError::UnprocessableEntity(errors));
        }
        
        Ok(Json(value))
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Path rendering with array indices
- [ ] Expected/received value display
- [ ] Nested error tree rendering
- [ ] Multiple error grouping
- [ ] Tree connector characters (├ └ │)
- [ ] Color coding (path=red, expected=green)
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with extractor validation
- [ ] Unit tests

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- bd-1hu2 (Phase 4 feature)

- Large arrays of errors should summarize

 {"id":"bd-33qj","title":"Implement visual regression tests","description":"## Task: Visual Regression Tests (REVISED)

### Overview
Implement snapshot-based visual regression testing using the ACTUAL implemented API to catch unintended changes to output formatting.

### Background
Visual regression testing ensures output stability:
- Catch formatting changes during refactoring
- Document expected output explicitly
- Easy to review changes in PRs

### Testing Approach with Actual API

## Task Overview

Create the facade.rs module containing the RichOutput struct - the central API that all
other parts of fastapi_rust will use for console output. This facade abstracts away the
mode selection and provides a unified interface.

## Design Rationale

### Why a Facade Pattern?

1. **Single Entry Point**: All output goes through one API
2. **Mode Abstraction**: Callers don't need to know about Rich/Plain/Minimal
3. **Testability**: Can inject mock RichOutput in tests
4. **Future-Proofing**: Can add new modes without changing call sites

### Why a Global Instance?

Output statements are scattered throughout the codebase:
- Startup in app.rs
- Logging in middleware
- Errors in error.rs
- Testing in testing.rs

Passing RichOutput through every function signature would be extremely invasive.
Therefore: lazy-initialized global instance, accessed via get_global().

## File: crates/fastapi-output/src/facade.rs

```rust
//\! RichOutput facade for unified console output.

use std::io::{self, Write};
use std::sync::{LazyLock, RwLock};
use std::cell::RefCell;

use crate::mode::OutputMode;
use crate::themes::FastApiTheme;
use crate::testing::TestOutput;

#[cfg(feature = "rich")]
use rich_rust::prelude::*;

/// Global RichOutput instance, lazily initialized.
static GLOBAL_OUTPUT: LazyLock<RwLock<RichOutput>> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    RwLock::new(RichOutput::auto())
});

/// Thread-local test output for capturing during tests.
thread_local\! {
    static TEST_OUTPUT: RefCell<Option<TestOutput>> = RefCell::new(None);
}

/// Get the global RichOutput instance.
pub fn get_global() -> impl std::ops::Deref<Target = RichOutput> + '_ {
    GLOBAL_OUTPUT.read().unwrap()
}

/// Replace the global RichOutput instance.
pub fn set_global(output: RichOutput) {
    *GLOBAL_OUTPUT.write().unwrap() = output;
}

/// Central facade for all console output.
pub struct RichOutput {
    mode: OutputMode,
    theme: FastApiTheme,
    
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    console: Option<Console>,
}

impl RichOutput {
    /// Create with automatic mode detection.
    pub fn auto() -> Self {
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] RichOutput::auto() called");
        Self::with_mode(OutputMode::auto())
    }
    
    /// Create with explicit plain mode (agent-compatible).
    pub fn plain() -> Self {
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] RichOutput::plain() created");
        Self::with_mode(OutputMode::Plain)
    }
    
    /// Create with explicit rich mode (human terminal).
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    pub fn rich() -> Self {
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] RichOutput::rich() created");
        Self::with_mode(OutputMode::Rich)
    }
    
    /// Create with specific mode.
    pub fn with_mode(mode: OutputMode) -> Self {
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] RichOutput::with_mode({:?}) created", mode);
        Self {
            mode,
            theme: FastApiTheme::default(),
            #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
            console: if mode == OutputMode::Rich {
                Some(Console::new())
            } else {
                None
            },
        }
    }
    
    /// Create a builder for custom configuration.
    pub fn builder() -> RichOutputBuilder {
        RichOutputBuilder::new()
    }
    
    /// Get the current output mode.
    pub fn mode(&self) -> OutputMode {
        self.mode
    }
    
    /// Get the current theme.
    pub fn theme(&self) -> &FastApiTheme {
        &self.theme
    }
    
    /// Run closure with test output capture.
    pub fn with_test_output<F: FnOnce()>(test_output: &TestOutput, f: F) {
        TEST_OUTPUT.with(|cell| {
            *cell.borrow_mut() = Some(test_output.clone());
        });
        f();
        TEST_OUTPUT.with(|cell| {
            *cell.borrow_mut() = None;
        });
    }
    
    // ========== Status Messages ==========
    
    /// Print a success message.
    pub fn success(&self, message: &str) {
        self.status(StatusKind::Success, message);
    }
    
    /// Print an error message.
    pub fn error(&self, message: &str) {
        self.status(StatusKind::Error, message);
    }
    
    /// Print a warning message.
    pub fn warning(&self, message: &str) {
        self.status(StatusKind::Warning, message);
    }
    
    /// Print an info message.
    pub fn info(&self, message: &str) {
        self.status(StatusKind::Info, message);
    }
    
    /// Print a debug message.
    pub fn debug(&self, message: &str) {
        self.status(StatusKind::Debug, message);
    }
    
    /// Print a status message with custom kind.
    pub fn status(&self, kind: StatusKind, message: &str) {
        let output = match self.mode {
            OutputMode::Rich => self.format_status_rich(kind, message),
            OutputMode::Plain => self.format_status_plain(kind, message),
            OutputMode::Minimal => self.format_status_minimal(kind, message),
        };
        self.write_line(&output);
    }
    
    fn format_status_plain(&self, kind: StatusKind, message: &str) -> String {
        format\!("{} {}", kind.plain_prefix(), message)
    }
    
    fn format_status_minimal(&self, kind: StatusKind, message: &str) -> String {
        format\!("{} {}", kind.plain_prefix(), message)
    }
    
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    fn format_status_rich(&self, kind: StatusKind, message: &str) -> String {
        let (icon, _style) = kind.rich_format(&self.theme);
        format\!("{} {}", icon, message)
    }
    
    #[cfg(not(feature = "rich"))]
    fn format_status_rich(&self, kind: StatusKind, message: &str) -> String {
        self.format_status_plain(kind, message)
    }
    
    // ========== Output Writing ==========
    
    fn write_line(&self, text: &str) {
        // Check for test capture
        let captured = TEST_OUTPUT.with(|cell| {
            if let Some(ref test_output) = *cell.borrow() {
                test_output.push_line(text);
                true
            } else {
                false
            }
        });
        
        if \!captured {
            println\!("{}", text);
        }
    }
    
    /// Print a horizontal rule/divider.
    pub fn rule(&self, title: Option<&str>) {
        let output = match self.mode {
            OutputMode::Rich => self.format_rule_rich(title),
            OutputMode::Plain | OutputMode::Minimal => self.format_rule_plain(title),
        };
        self.write_line(&output);
    }
    
    fn format_rule_plain(&self, title: Option<&str>) -> String {
        match title {
            Some(t) => format\!("--- {} ---", t),
            None => "---".to_string(),
        }
    }
    
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    fn format_rule_rich(&self, _title: Option<&str>) -> String {
        // Would use console.rule() in real implementation
        self.format_rule_plain(_title)
    }
    
    #[cfg(not(feature = "rich"))]
    fn format_rule_rich(&self, title: Option<&str>) -> String {
        self.format_rule_plain(title)
    }
    
    /// Print content in a panel/box.
    pub fn panel(&self, content: &str, title: Option<&str>) {
        let output = match self.mode {
            OutputMode::Rich => self.format_panel_rich(content, title),
            OutputMode::Plain | OutputMode::Minimal => self.format_panel_plain(content, title),
        };
        self.write_line(&output);
    }
    
    fn format_panel_plain(&self, content: &str, title: Option<&str>) -> String {
        match title {
            Some(t) => format\!("[{}]\n{}", t, content),
            None => content.to_string(),
        }
    }
    
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    fn format_panel_rich(&self, content: &str, title: Option<&str>) -> String {
        self.format_panel_plain(content, title)
    }
    
    #[cfg(not(feature = "rich"))]
    fn format_panel_rich(&self, content: &str, title: Option<&str>) -> String {
        self.format_panel_plain(content, title)
    }
    
    /// Print raw text.
    pub fn print(&self, text: &str) {
        self.write_line(text);
    }
}

/// Builder for RichOutput with custom configuration.
pub struct RichOutputBuilder {
    mode: Option<OutputMode>,
    theme: Option<FastApiTheme>,
}

impl RichOutputBuilder {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { mode: None, theme: None }
    }
    
    pub fn mode(mut self, mode: OutputMode) -> Self {
        self.mode = Some(mode);
        self
    }
    
    pub fn theme(mut self, theme: FastApiTheme) -> Self {
        self.theme = Some(theme);
        self
    }
    
    pub fn build(self) -> RichOutput {
        let mode = self.mode.unwrap_or_else(OutputMode::auto);
        let mut output = RichOutput::with_mode(mode);
        if let Some(theme) = self.theme {
            output.theme = theme;
        }
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] RichOutputBuilder::build() mode={:?}", output.mode);
        output
    }
}

impl Default for RichOutputBuilder {
    fn default() -> Self { Self::new() }
}

/// Status message kinds.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum StatusKind {
    Success, Error, Warning, Info, Debug, Pending, InProgress,
}

impl StatusKind {
    pub fn plain_prefix(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Success => "[OK]",
            Self::Error => "[ERROR]",
            Self::Warning => "[WARN]",
            Self::Info => "[INFO]",
            Self::Debug => "[DEBUG]",
            Self::Pending => "[PENDING]",
            Self::InProgress => "[...]",
        }
    }
    
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    pub fn rich_format(&self, theme: &FastApiTheme) -> (&'static str, String) {
        match self {
            Self::Success => ("✓", format\!("bold {}", theme.success_hex())),
            Self::Error => ("✗", format\!("bold {}", theme.error_hex())),
            Self::Warning => ("⚠", format\!("bold {}", theme.warning_hex())),
            Self::Info => ("ℹ", format\!("bold {}", theme.info_hex())),
            Self::Debug => ("●", "dim".to_string()),
            Self::Pending => ("○", "dim".to_string()),
            Self::InProgress => ("◐", format\!("bold {}", theme.accent_hex())),
        }
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-39h5","title":"Implement routing debug output","description":"## Task: Routing Debug Output

### Parent Feature
Phase 4: HTTP & Router Integration (bd-1hu2)

### Overview
Create a visual debugging tool that shows the routing decision process, helping developers understand why routes match or fail.

### Background & Reasoning
Route matching bugs are common and frustrating:
- Route not found when it should exist
- Wrong route matching (order issues)
- Parameter extraction failures
- Method mismatch confusion

A routing debugger that shows the decision tree eliminates guesswork.

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/routing_debug.rs

pub struct RoutingDebugInfo {
    pub request_method: Method,
    pub request_path: String,
    pub candidates: Vec<RouteCandidate>,
    pub matched_route: Option<MatchedRoute>,
    pub match_duration: Duration,
}

pub struct RouteCandidate {
    pub pattern: String,
    pub method: Method,
    pub match_result: MatchResult,
}

pub enum MatchResult {
    Matched { params: Vec<(String, String)> },
    MethodMismatch { expected: Method },
    PathMismatch { reason: String },
    NotConsidered,
}

pub struct MatchedRoute {
    pub pattern: String,
    pub handler_name: String,
    pub params: Vec<(String, String)>,
    pub middleware: Vec<String>,
}
```

### Implementation
```rust
impl RoutingDebugInfo {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        // Request line
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!(
            "  Request: {} {}",
            theme.style_method(&self.request_method),
            self.request_path
        ));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // No match warning
        if self.matched_route.is_none() {
            content.push(theme.style_text("  ⚠ NO MATCH FOUND", "yellow bold"));
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        // Candidates table
        content.push(theme.style_text("  Candidates Evaluated", "bold"));
        
        for candidate in &self.candidates {
            let (icon, style, detail) = match &candidate.match_result {
                MatchResult::Matched { .. } => ("✓", "green", "MATCHED".to_string()),
                MatchResult::MethodMismatch { expected } => 
                    ("✗", "red", format!("method!={:?}", self.request_method)),
                MatchResult::PathMismatch { reason } => 
                    ("✗", "red", reason.clone()),
                MatchResult::NotConsidered => 
                    ("·", "dim", "not evaluated".to_string()),
            };
            
            content.push(format!(
                "  {} {:6} {:25} {}",
                theme.style_text(icon, style),
                format!("{:?}", candidate.method),
                candidate.pattern,
                theme.style_text(&detail, "dim")
            ));
        }
        
        // Match result details
        if let Some(matched) = &self.matched_route {
            content.push(String::new());
            content.push(theme.style_text("  Match Result", "bold"));
            content.push(format!("  ├── Pattern: {}", matched.pattern));
            content.push(format!("  ├── Handler: {}", matched.handler_name));
            
            let params_str = matched.params.iter()
                .map(|(k, v)| format!("{}={}", k, v))
                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
                .join(", ");
            content.push(format!("  ├── Params:  {}", 
                if params_str.is_empty() { "(none)".to_string() } else { params_str }));
            
            content.push(format!("  └── Middleware: {:?}", matched.middleware));
        }
        
        // Timing
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(theme.style_text(
            &format!("  Match time: {:.3}ms", self.match_duration.as_micros() as f64 / 1000.0),
            "dim"
        ));
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("Route Matching")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
}
```

### Integration with Router
```rust
// In fastapi-router/src/lib.rs

impl Router {
    #[cfg(feature = "debug-output")]
    pub fn match_with_debug(&self, method: Method, path: &str) -> (Option<RouteMatch>, RoutingDebugInfo) {
        let start = Instant::now();
        let mut candidates = Vec::new();
        
        for route in &self.routes {
            let result = self.check_match(route, method, path);
            candidates.push(RouteCandidate {
                pattern: route.pattern.clone(),
                method: route.method,
                match_result: result,
            });
        }
        
        let matched = candidates.iter()
            .find(|c| matches!(c.match_result, MatchResult::Matched { .. }));
        
        let debug_info = RoutingDebugInfo {
            request_method: method,
            request_path: path.to_string(),
            candidates,
            matched_route: matched.map(|m| /* ... */),
            match_duration: start.elapsed(),
        };
        
        (matched.map(|m| m.into()), debug_info)
    }
}
```
